experiment: H_MOTIF_BASIS — Local motif basis compression across software and social graphs
description: Confirmatory v1 test of H_MOTIF_BASIS on unseen datasets. Meta-dataset compares local motif-basis metrics derived from 1-hop egonet signatures across two software multilayer graphs and two social multilayer graphs.

hypotheses.statement: Engineered software graphs are more symbolically compressible than social graphs: software_mean motif_compression_ratio is lower than social_mean motif_compression_ratio.
WordPress multilayer architecture has lower motif_compression_ratio than the organizational social multilayer graph.
The generic software multilayer graph has lower motif_compression_ratio than social_v2.
Social graphs require a larger local motif vocabulary than software graphs: social_mean motif_basis_ratio is higher than software_mean motif_basis_ratio.

notes: Metric definition fixed before running: collapse graph to undirected simple graph, compute each node's 1-hop egonet degree-sequence signature, define motif basis as the set of unique signatures, and compression ratio as |M|/|E|. Exploratory probe on a separate trio (linux/social/random) was used only to choose the metric, not the confirmatory datasets used here.
